    <h1 {...{
      "id": "launch-external-processes"
    }}>{`Launch External Processes`}</h1>
    <p>{`Open files and launch applications from your plugin to extend workflows beyond Premiere`}</p>
    <p>{`UXP provides the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/shell/Shell.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`shell`}</inlineCode>{` API`}</a>{` to let your plugin interact with the user's operating system—`}<strong parentName="p">{`opening files`}</strong>{` in their default applications and `}<strong parentName="p">{`launching external programs`}</strong>{` via URL schemes. This is useful for workflows that span across multiple tools.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "system-requirements"
    }}>{`System requirements`}</h2>
    <p>{`Please make make sure your development environment uses the following `}<strong parentName="p">{`minimum versions`}</strong>{` to avoid compatibility issues:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Premiere v25.6`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`UDT v2.2`}</strong></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Manifest v5`}</strong></li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "launch-process-security"
    }}>{`Launch Process Security`}</h2>
    <p>{`By default, UXP plugins can't launch external processes; this protects users from unwanted or malicious activity. If your plugin needs to open files or launch applications, `}<strong parentName="p">{`you must declare`}</strong>{` the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../plugins/concepts/manifest/index.md#launchprocesspermission"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`launchProcess`}</inlineCode></a>{` permission in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`. It has two components:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Extensions`}</strong>{`: file extensions your plugin can open, for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`[".pdf", ".txt"]`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Schemes`}</strong>{`: URL schemes your plugin can use, for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`["https", "mailto"]`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "launchProcess": {
      "extensions": [".pdf", ".mp4"],
      "schemes": ["https", "mailto"]
    }
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`User consent is always required`}</p>
    <p>{`Whenever your plugin attempts to launch an external process, `}<strong parentName="p">{`the user must provide explicit consent`}</strong>{` through a system dialog. Always provide clear, helpful context in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`developerText`}</inlineCode>{` parameter to explain why the action is needed.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "using-the-shell-api"
    }}>{`Using the Shell API`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../uxp-api/reference-js/Modules/uxp/shell/Shell.md"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`shell`}</inlineCode></a>{` module provides two main methods:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`openPath()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Opens a file or folder in the system's default application.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`openExternal()`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Launches an application using a URL scheme.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Both methods require user consent and return a `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise"
      }}>{`Promise`}</a>{` that resolves with an empty string on success or an error message on failure.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Access the API:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { shell } = require("uxp");
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-open-with-default-application"
    }}>{`Example: Open with Default Application`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`openPath()`}</inlineCode>{` to open any file on the user's system in its default application. For example, PDFs in a reader, videos in a player, or text files in an editor.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { shell } = require("uxp");

// Open a PDF file in the default PDF reader 📄
async function openPDFFile() {
  try {
    // For macOS
    const result = await shell.openPath(
      "/Users/user/Desktop/report.pdf", // 👈 update with your path
      "Opening project report for review"
    );
    // For Windows, use: "C:\\\\Users\\\\user\\\\Desktop\\\\report.pdf"

    if (result === "") {
      console.log("✅ File opened successfully");
    } else {
      console.error(\`❌ Failed to open file: \${result}\`);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Error opening file:", err);
  }
}

// Open a folder in Finder/Explorer 📁
async function openProjectFolder() {
  // 👇 Must have an empty string "" to allow opening folders
  // in the Manifest requiredPermissions.launchProcess.extensions array
  try {
    // For macOS
    const result = await shell.openPath(
      "/Users/user/Documents/Projects",
      "Opening project folder"
    );
    // For Windows, use: "C:\\\\Users\\\\user\\\\Documents\\\\Projects"

    if (result === "") {
      console.log("✅ Folder opened successfully");
    } else {
      console.error(\`❌ Failed to open folder: \${result}\`);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Error opening folder:", err);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>

}
`}</code></pre>

Use an `}<strong parentName="p">{`empty string`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`""`}</inlineCode>{` to allow `}<strong parentName="p">{`opening folders`}</strong>{` in Finder/Explorer, as in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`openProjectFolder()`}</inlineCode>{` function of the above example.`}</p>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-launch-application-with-url-scheme"
    }}>{`Example: Launch Application with URL Scheme`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`openExternal()`}</inlineCode>{` to launch applications via URL schemes—open websites in browsers, compose emails, or trigger platform-specific apps like Maps.`}</p>
    <CodeBlock slots="heading, code" repeat="2" languages="JavaScript, JSON" mdxType="CodeBlock" />
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "indexjs-1"
    }}>{`index.js`}</h4>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { shell } = require("uxp");

// Open a website in the default browser 🌐
async function openDocumentation() {
  try {
    const result = await shell.openExternal(
      "https://developer.adobe.com/",
      "Opening Adobe Developer documentation"
    );

    if (result === "") {
      console.log("✅ Browser opened successfully");
    } else {
      console.error(\`❌ Failed to open browser: \${result}\`);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Error opening browser:", err);
  }
}

// Compose an email with pre-filled content 📧
async function sendFeedbackEmail() {
  try {
    const subject = encodeURIComponent("Plugin Feedback");
    const body = encodeURIComponent("I have feedback about your plugin...");

    const result = await shell.openExternal(
      \`mailto:support@example.com?subject=\${subject}&body=\${body}\`,
      "Opening mail client to send feedback"
    );

    if (result === "") {
      console.log("✅ Mail client opened successfully");
    } else {
      console.error(\`❌ Failed to open mail client: \${result}\`);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Error opening mail client:", err);
  }
}

// Open Maps to a specific location 🗺️
async function openLocationInMaps() {
  try {
    // For macOS: use maps:// scheme
    const macResult = await shell.openExternal(
      "maps://?address=345+Park+Ave+San+Jose",
      "Opening Maps to Adobe office location"
    );

    // For Windows: use bingmaps: scheme
    // const winResult = await shell.openExternal(
    //   "bingmaps:?q=345+Park+Ave+San+Jose,+95110",
    //   "Opening Maps to Adobe office location"
    // );

    if (macResult === "") {
      console.log("✅ Maps opened successfully");
    } else {
      console.error(\`❌ Failed to open Maps: \${macResult}\`);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Error opening Maps:", err);
  }
}
`}</code></pre>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "run-cli-commands"
    }}>{`Run CLI commands`}</h3>
    <p>{`It is possible to run CLI commands (like AppleScripts or shell scripts) directly from your plugin using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`shell.openPath()`}</inlineCode>{`. There are known limitations with this approach; for example, it's not possible to pass any parameters, nor to capture the output of the command:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const result = await shell.openPath(    // external command
  "/bin/ls",                            // ✅ will run
  "Running the ls command"              // ⚠️ result is an empty string
);

const result = await shell.openPath(    // external command + argument
  "/bin/ls -la",                        // ❌ will fail
  "Running the ls -la command"
);
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Provide clear context`}</strong>{`: The `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`developerText`}</inlineCode>{` parameter appears in the user consent dialog. Write clear, user-friendly explanations in plain language.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`// ✅ Good: Clear and specific
await shell.openExternal(
  "https://example.com/guide",
  "Opening tutorial guide in your browser"
);

// ❌ Avoid: Vague or technical
await shell.openExternal(
  "https://example.com/guide",
  "Opening URL"
);
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 2
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Handle user denial gracefully`}</strong>{`: Users can deny the launch request. Check the return value and provide fallback options.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const result = await shell.openPath(
  filePath,
  "Opening project file"
);
if (result !== "") {
  // User denied or operation failed
  console.log("Unable to open file. Please open it manually.");
}
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 3
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Check platform compatibility`}</strong>{`: Use platform detection for platform-specific schemes.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const isMac = require("os").platform() === "darwin";
const scheme = isMac ? "maps://" : "bingmaps:";
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 4
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Encode URL parameters`}</strong>{`: When building URLs with query parameters, always encode special characters.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const subject = encodeURIComponent("My Subject");
const url = \`mailto:user@example.com?subject=\${subject}\`;
`}</code></pre>
    <ol {...{
      "start": 5
    }}>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Declare all schemes and extensions`}</strong>{`: Only list the schemes and extensions your plugin actually uses—don't request unnecessary permissions.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "troubleshoot-common-issues"
    }}>{`Troubleshoot Common Issues`}</h2>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Symptom`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Likely Cause`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Solution`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">

            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Permission denied error`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Missing manifest entry`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Add the extension or scheme to `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`launchProcess`}</inlineCode>{` in manifest`}</td>

        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Operation fails silently`}</td>

            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Check return value and handle denial gracefully`}</td>

          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Platform-specific scheme not working`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Wrong scheme for OS`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Use platform detection to choose the correct scheme`}</td>

        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`file://`}</inlineCode>{` scheme doesn't work`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Wrong method used`}</td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": "left"
          }}>{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`openPath()`}</inlineCode>{` for local files, not `}<inlineCode parentName="td">{`openExternal()`}</inlineCode></td>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "reference-material"
    }}>{`Reference Material`}</h2>
    <ul>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Launch Process Security`}</strong>{`: By default, plugins cannot launch external processes. You must declare the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`launchProcess`}</inlineCode>{` permission in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`extensions`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Array of file extensions your plugin can open (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`[".pdf", ".mp4"]`}</inlineCode>{`). Use an empty string `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`""`}</inlineCode>{` to allow opening folders in Finder/Explorer.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`schemes`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Array of URL schemes your plugin can use (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`["https", "mailto"]`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`User consent is `}<strong parentName="li">{`always required`}</strong>{` for security—users see a dialog for every launch attempt unless the choice is remembered.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Two Shell Methods`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`openPath(path, developerText)`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Opens files or folders in their default system application; requires the file extension to be listed in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`extensions`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`openExternal(url, developerText)`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Launches applications via URL schemes; requires the scheme to be listed in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`schemes`}</inlineCode>{`; cannot use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`file://`}</inlineCode>{` (use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`openPath()`}</inlineCode>{` instead).`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`User Experience Best Practices`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul">{`URL schemes are operating system-specific: use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`require("os").platform()`}</inlineCode>{` to check the operating system and use the appropriate scheme.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Provide clear, user-friendly text in the `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`developerText`}</inlineCode>{` parameter to explain why the action is needed.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Handle user denial gracefully by checking return values and offering fallback options.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Only declare schemes and extensions your plugin actually uses—avoid requesting unnecessary permissions.`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul">{`Encode URL parameters with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`encodeURIComponent()`}</inlineCode>{` to handle special characters properly.`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
